game_text: At the start of your turn, generate 1 Infrastructure resource. If this Asset is destroyed, Blue Team loses 1 SCP.
flavor_text: The smallest source can become the most important asset.
learning_note: Wells are critical physical assets that should be included in cyber and operational risk planning.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Scenario Asset. At the end of turn 6, if this Asset is active, Blue Team gains 2 SCP.
flavor_text: Public pressure rises when the tower is at risk.
learning_note: Water storage assets connect cyber events to visible community impact.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Once per turn, move 1 Defense counter from this Asset to another Infrastructure Asset.
flavor_text: Flow control is resilience in motion.
learning_note: Pump stations are operational choke points and should be protected as critical assets.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: The first time this Asset takes damage each turn, discard 1 card unless Field Technician or Infrastructure Engineer is active.
flavor_text: Safety depends on more than uptime.
learning_note: Chemical treatment systems require strong monitoring because integrity failures can affect safety.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Protocol cards attached to this Asset cost 1 less resource to play.
flavor_text: Small controllers carry big consequences.
learning_note: RTUs and PLCs bridge cyber systems and physical operations in ICS environments.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: The first Internet Attack against this Asset deals 1 less damage.
flavor_text: Every path in needs a guardrail.
learning_note: Gateways and remote access paths should be monitored and hardened.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: If Network Monitoring is active, the first Network Attack against this Asset each turn deals 1 less damage.
flavor_text: The wireless edge is still part of the plant.
learning_note: Wireless access points should be inventoried, segmented, and monitored because they can bridge IT and OT environments.

game_text: When you play a Protocol card, you may restore 1 Defense counter to one Infrastructure Asset.
flavor_text: Engineering access changes real-world behavior.
learning_note: Engineering workstations are high-value systems because they can view or modify industrial control logic and configurations.

game_text: Scenario Asset. At the end of turn 8, if this Asset is active, Blue Team gains 3 SCP. If destroyed, Blue Team loses 2 SCP.
flavor_text: The plant is where cyber becomes public safety.
learning_note: Treatment plants concentrate operational risk and should receive layered protection, monitoring, and recovery planning.

game_text: If revealed by Recon, Red Team may draw 1 card unless MFA is active.
flavor_text: A useful dashboard can become a useful target.
learning_note: Exposed SCADA interfaces increase risk when authentication and access control are weak.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: If an Operator is active, this Asset gains +1 Defense.
flavor_text: The laptop is small. The access is not.
learning_note: Operator endpoints often become high-value targets because they touch operational systems.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Internet Attacks cost Red Team 1 additional resource. If IDS is active, Network Attacks against this Asset deal 1 less damage.
flavor_text: Segmentation buys time.
learning_note: Firewalls and segmentation reduce attacker movement between enterprise IT and operational environments.

game_text: Credential Attacks against this Asset deal +1 damage unless MFA is active. If damaged, Blue Team discards 1 card.
flavor_text: Customer data became operational pressure.
learning_note: Business systems can affect utility operations through billing, communications, and customer trust.

game_text: Delivery Attacks against Operators cost Red Team 1 less while this Asset is active. MFA cancels this bonus.
flavor_text: Every inbox is a doorway.
learning_note: Email infrastructure is a common delivery path for phishing, malware, and credential theft.

game_text: Restore up to 2 Defense counters to one damaged Infrastructure Asset.
flavor_text: Recovery starts before the incident.
learning_note: Backups support resilience only when they are tested, protected, and available during response.

game_text: Restore up to 3 Defense counters to one damaged Asset. If Backup is in your discard pile, return Backup to your hand.
flavor_text: One copy is a hope. Redundancy is a plan.
learning_note: Redundant backups reduce recovery risk when local systems are encrypted, damaged, or unavailable.

game_text: Reduce damage from Credential Attack or Password Spray by 1 and cancel that card's bonus effect. If Remote Access Tool is attached to the targeted Asset, detach it.
flavor_text: Something you know, something you have, something you are.
learning_note: MFA helps reduce risk from guessed, reused, or stolen credentials.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Cancel one Attack that targets Chlorination Unit or Treatment Plant, or reduce its damage by 3.
flavor_text: The sensor noticed what the screen missed.
learning_note: Independent monitoring can detect unsafe process changes even when cyber systems are degraded.

game_text: Reveal the top card of the Red Team deck. If it is a Recon card, draw 1 card. Then put the revealed card back on top or on the bottom of the Red Team deck. Discard Port Scanner after use.
flavor_text: Defenders can scan too.
learning_note: Authorized scanning helps validate exposure and find services before attackers do.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Reveal the top 3 Red Team cards. You may discard one Exploit card revealed this way.
flavor_text: Known weaknesses are easier to fix before pressure arrives.
learning_note: Vulnerability scanning helps prioritize patching and exposure reduction across IT and OT support systems.

game_text: The next Command and Control or Install card Red Team plays costs 1 additional resource. Draw 1 card if Deep Packet ICS Monitor is active.
flavor_text: A quiet alert can stop a loud incident.
learning_note: Intrusion detection helps spot attacker behavior before it becomes operational impact.

game_text: Reveal one hidden Asset or Protocol. If it is Internet or Network realm, Port Closure costs 0 this turn.
flavor_text: Small devices leave big footprints.
learning_note: IoT and OT asset discovery helps defenders find unmanaged devices and reduce blind spots.

game_text: Attach to RTU/PLC. That Asset gains +1 Defense but takes +1 damage from Recon-followed Attacks.
flavor_text: Simple protocols need careful boundaries.
learning_note: Legacy ICS protocols may lack modern security features and require compensating controls.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Attach to an Application or Internet Asset. That Asset gains +1 Defense. If revealed by Recon, Red Team may draw 1 card.
flavor_text: The safe-looking port still needs rules.
learning_note: Encrypted web services still need access control, logging, and proper segmentation.

game_text: Attach to SCADA Web Interface, Email Server, or Billing Database. That Asset gains +1 Defense. Public Info Gathering does not reveal it automatically.
flavor_text: Encryption helps, but it is not authorization.
learning_note: HTTPS protects data in transit but does not replace authentication, authorization, or patching.

game_text: Attach to RTU/PLC or Treatment Plant. That Asset gains +1 Defense, but Recon against it may reveal one additional Asset.
flavor_text: Old signals still carry critical commands.
learning_note: DNP3 and similar industrial protocols require segmentation, monitoring, and secure deployment practices.

game_text: Once per turn, reduce Network Attack damage by 1.
flavor_text: Someone has to know where the cables go.
learning_note: IT administrators support segmentation, account control, and response during incidents.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Once per turn, reduce damage to an Infrastructure Asset by 1. When you play DNP3 or Modbus Protocol, draw 1 card.
flavor_text: The engineer knows which alarm matters.
learning_note: OT engineers connect cyber response to safe physical process decisions.

game_text: When Red Team plays Recon or Command and Control, reveal the top Red Team card. You may put it on the bottom of the deck.
flavor_text: The alert became a decision.
learning_note: SOC analysts help turn telemetry into timely response and containment.

game_text: Restore 1 Defense to one Infrastructure Asset each turn.
flavor_text: Boots on the ground keep water moving.
learning_note: Field staff are essential to physical validation and manual recovery during cyber incidents.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Provides 1 Infrastructure resource.
flavor_text: Keeping the lights on is a strategy.
learning_note: Operations funding supports routine maintenance and daily resilience.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Provides 1 Infrastructure resource. If used for an Asset, that Asset enters with +1 Defense.
flavor_text: Big upgrades start with rare funding.
learning_note: Capital funding helps replace or improve critical systems.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Provides 2 resources for a Respond or Recover card. Discard after use.
flavor_text: Emergency money is only useful when the plan exists.
learning_note: Emergency funding helps utilities respond quickly but works best when response procedures are prepared.

game_text: Provides 2 Infrastructure resources for a Defense, Tool, or Recovery card. Discard after use.
flavor_text: Help arrives when preparation meets need.
learning_note: External funding and assistance can improve recovery and resilience for small utilities.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Cancel one Credential Attack or restore 2 Defense to one Operator or Endpoint Asset.
flavor_text: Regaining control starts with the account.
learning_note: Account recovery is part of containment when credentials are suspected to be compromised.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Attach to one Endpoint Asset. The first time Pre-Built Malware Package would damage that Asset each turn, reduce that damage by 2. You may discard Antivirus to remove one Remote Access Tool attached to that Asset.
flavor_text: Basic protection is better than wishful thinking.
learning_note: Endpoint protection is a baseline control that reduces common malware and remote-access risk, but it should not be the only control.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Cancel one Recon card or reduce one Network Attack by 2 damage.
flavor_text: Closed ports tell fewer stories.
learning_note: Reducing unnecessary exposed services limits attacker options.
source_note: Shared card from Small Org; keep text/cost consistent.

game_text: Cancel one Command and Control card unless Red Team pays 1 additional Network resource.
flavor_text: The packets told the truth.
learning_note: Packet analysis helps validate suspicious traffic and distinguish normal operations from attacker behavior.

game_text: The first Network Attack each turn deals 1 less damage. If IDS is active, draw 1 card when Red Team plays Recon.
flavor_text: Visibility turns surprise into response.
learning_note: Network monitoring gives defenders early warning and supports incident investigation.

game_text: Look at the top 2 Red Team cards. You may reveal one Command and Control card and discard it.
flavor_text: Capture first. Guess later.
learning_note: Packet captures provide evidence for response tuning and post-incident lessons learned.
